zotero.abstractNote: We present HMDˆ2, a novel framework for generating full-body human motion from a single egocentric head-mounted device (HMD). Existing methods for egocentric motion capture often struggle with the limited field-of-view of HMD cameras, leading to ambiguous poses and lack of interaction with the environment. HMDˆ2 addresses these challenges by leveraging a large-scale dataset of egocentric videos and a novel environment-aware motion generation model. Our model incorporates a spatial-temporal transformer architecture that effectively integrates egocentric visual features with environmental context, enabling the generation of physically plausible and environment-aware motions. We demonstrate that HMDˆ2 significantly outperforms state-of-the-art methods in terms of motion accuracy and environmental consistency, particularly in complex, cluttered scenes.
